title: Sagenia decurrens
taxonomy: Sagenia decurrens is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This genus is part of the family of deciduous ferns. Sagenia decurrens is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Sagenia decurrens places it in the phylum Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. It is also known to belong to the family Thelypteridaceae, along with other genera.

intro: Sagenia decurrens, also known as the Cape daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Cape Province of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sagenia decurrens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Sagenia decurrens is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal tinctures and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
